task: Send a text message using Simple SMS Messenger to +14632272737 with message: Hello, World!

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages (Google), Chrome, and another app with an 'M' icon. The target app, "Simple SMS Messenger", is not visible among the shortcuts on this screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open Simple SMS Messenger app → | Step 2: Start a new message | Step 3: Enter recipient number +14632272737 | Step 4: Type message "Hello, World!" and send

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the target app "Simple SMS Messenger" is not on the home screen shortcuts. To proceed with sending the text message, I need to launch the app first. Since it's not visible, I will use a system-level open command to launch "Simple SMS Messenger" directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the main interface of Simple SMS Messenger. It displays "No stored conversations have been found" and a text link "Start a conversation". There is also a prominent floating action button (FAB) with a '+' icon in the bottom right corner.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple SMS Messenger app ✓ | Step 2: Start a new message → | Step 3: Enter recipient number +14632272737 | Step 4: Type message "Hello, World!" and send

[Decision]
Based on the current screen state and the plan's current step to start a new message, I need to initiate a new conversation. The screen provides two ways to do this: the "Start a conversation" text link in the middle and the '+' FAB button in the bottom right corner. The '+' button is the standard UI element for creating a new item in this app's design. I will tap the '+' button to proceed.

[Memory]
Target recipient: +14632272737. Target message: "Hello, World!". App successfully opened, ready to compose a new message.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "New conversation" interface. There is an input field labeled "Add Contact or Number..." at the top, which is currently active with the keyboard displayed below. The screen says "No contacts found" below the input field.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple SMS Messenger app ✓ | Step 2: Start a new message ✓ | Step 3: Enter recipient number +14632272737 → | Step 4: Type message "Hello, World!" and send

[Decision]
Based on the current plan step to enter the recipient number, and observing the "Add Contact or Number..." input field is already focused and ready for text entry, I need to type the target phone number into this field. I will input "+14632272737" as specified in the memory.

[Memory]
Target recipient: +14632272737. Target message: "Hello, World!". Ready to compose the message after recipient is set.

[Observation]
The current screen displays the "New conversation" interface with the phone number "+14632272737" entered in the recipient field. To the right of the entered number, there is a prominent checkmark icon. The keyboard is still visible on the screen. The message "No contacts found" is displayed below, which is expected since it's a direct number input.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed that entering the number would immediately transition to typing the message, but I need to confirm the entered number first by tapping the checkmark before the conversation screen opens.

[Replan]
Step 1-2: Open app and start new message ✓ | Step 3: Enter recipient number ✓ | Step 4: Confirm recipient → | Step 5: Type message "Hello, World!" and send

[Decision]
Based on the Replan's current step to confirm the recipient, I observe the checkmark icon next to the entered number "+14632272737". I need to tap this checkmark to finalize the recipient selection and proceed to the actual message composition screen.

[Memory]
Target recipient: +14632272737 (entered). Target message: "Hello, World!". Need to transition to the chat interface next.

[Observation]
The screen displays the conversation interface with "+14632272737" at the top. The keyboard is open, and there is a text input field at the bottom labeled "Type a message...". The "SMS" send button is visible to the right of the input field.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-3: Open app, start new message, enter recipient ✓ | Step 4: Confirm recipient ✓ | Step 5: Type message "Hello, World!" and send →

[Decision]
Based on the Plan Update, the current step is to type the message "Hello, World!". I am on the correct conversation screen with the input field ready. I will input the text "Hello, World!" into the "Type a message..." field.

[Memory]
Target recipient: +14632272737 (confirmed). Target message: "Hello, World!" (ready to type). Next step will be sending the message after typing.

[Observation]
The screen shows the SMS conversation interface with "+14632272737" at the top. The text "Hello, World!" has been successfully typed into the input field at the bottom. The keyboard is still visible, and there is a send button (a paper airplane icon with "SMS" below it) on the right side of the input field.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Open app, enter recipient ✓ | Step 5: Type message "Hello, World!" ✓ | Step 6: Send the message →

[Decision]
Based on the Plan Update, the current step is to send the message. The text "Hello, World!" is already in the input field, and the send button (paper airplane icon) is clearly visible on the right side of the text box. I will tap this button to send the SMS.

[Memory]
Target recipient: +14632272737 (confirmed). Target message: "Hello, World!" (typed). Next action is sending to complete the task.

[Observation]
The screen shows the SMS conversation interface with "+14632272737". The message "Hello, World!" appears in a blue bubble on the right side of the screen, indicating it has been sent. There is a checkmark below the message bubble confirming delivery. The input field is now empty.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Open app, enter recipient ✓ | Step 5: Type message "Hello, World!" ✓ | Step 6: Send the message ✓ | Step 7: Verify task completion →

[Decision]
Based on the Observation, the message "Hello, World!" has been successfully sent to the correct recipient "+14632272737", as evidenced by the sent message bubble. All requirements of the original task (send a text message using Simple SMS Messenger to +14632272737 with message "Hello, World!") have been fulfilled. Therefore, I will declare the task complete.
